title_en: 224. They do not have the entire hadith but it is the hadith ofWaabisah.
title_id: Hadis #27: Kebaikan adalah Akhlak yang Baik (lanjutan)
content_en: 6 lbn Muhammad, p. 165. His complete discussion (as relied upon here) is found on pp. 165-1 67. 7 Al-Hilaali, Eeqaadh, p. 375. Commentary on the Forty Hadith of al-Nawawi General Comments About the Hadith This is another important and comprehensive hadith of the Prophet (peace be upon him), wherein he has explained the essence of righteousness and sinfulness. About the Narrator (I): al-Nawwaas ibn Samaan Al-Nawwaas ibn Samaan was a Companion of the Prophet (peace be upon him). He, accompanying his father, met the Prophet (peace be upon him) with a delegation of his people. He lived in Madinah for one year to learn from the Prophet (peace be upon him). He was one of the ahl al-Suffah (the poor Muslims who lived in the Prophet's mosque). In Sahih Muslim he is recorded to have said that the only thing that kept him from emigrating to Madinah was his need or want to ask the Prophet (peace be upon him) questions. Those who emigrated to Madinah did not ask the Prophet (peace be upon him) many questions due to the general disapproval of asking too many questions. Hence, only those who came from outside Madinah were free to put questions to the Prophet (peace be upon him). After the death of the Prophet (peace be upon him) he lived in Greater Syria. Seventeen hadith have been recorded on his authority. Muslim recorded three of them. About the Narrator (II): Waabisah ibn Mabad Waabisah ibn Mabad and visited the Prophet as part of a delegation in the year 9 A.H. He was well-known for his gentleness and his tendency to cry a great deal. Eleven hadith have been recorded on his authority. "Righteousness is good character." lbn Uthaimeen explains this portion of the hadith to mean that good character is one aspect of righteousness. 1 However, this does not seem to capture the essence of the Prophet's statement. It seems, instead, that the meaning of this phrase is similar to the meaning of the phrases discussed earlier, "The pilgrimage is Arafah," and "The religion is naseehah." The meaning here is, "The main or essential aspect of righteousness is good character. "2 As noted below, depending on how one understands the meaning of the word birr ("righteousness"), the hadith could be left in its literal sense. The 1 lbn Uthaimeen, Sharh Riyaadh, vol. 6, p. 1 83. 2 Cf., ibn Daqeeq al-Eid, Sharh, p. 72. Hadith #27: "Righteousness is Good Character .. " religion is truly and completely "good character" in the sense explained below.1 The Meaning of al-Birr ("Righteousness") The word birr is used in two senses. The first is treating others in a good fashion. In particular, one should treat one's parents in a good manner. This is known as birr al-waalidain. The word, though, is used in a more general sense to behave well toward anyone, not just one's parents. Sometimes the word birr is used in conjunction with the word taqwa. For example, Allah says in the Quran, ... -.:__s) a:1\j N1 Á i)jL;.Jj "Help you one another in al-birr and al-taqwa" (al-Maaidah 2). lbn Rajah states that in this verse al-birr refers to dealing with others while al-taqwa refers to dealing properly with Allah by obeying His commands.2 If this is the usage intended by Prophet (peace be upon him) in this particular statement, then the hadith must be understood to mean, "Good character is essential to righteous dealings with others." The second sense in which birr is used refers to all acts of worship and obedience to Allah, both inward and outward. This usage is found in the following verse of the Quran, ... ,., .... ., ,,,,., ... ...,., J. 0 - ,.,. ,,, J ,,,. ;:ii :_; Ɵ\ Âj yÃlj JϠI ύ Ѩ 1_,J'jJ ,) Ɵ\ ਲ਼ / .... ,,,.,,. 1 The importance of good character has been discussed in the commentary to Hadith Number 1 7. The attempt here will be to stress or discuss what was not discussed earlier. By now the reader has probably already noted how many or the hadith in this small collection are related to the behavior and character of a believer. This should not come as a surprise. In fact, ibn al-Qayyim once stated, "All of the religion is behavior and character (khuluq). What increases you in [good] behavior, increases you in your religion." See ibn al-Qayyim, Madaarij, vol. 2, p. 294. It is important to note that ibn al-Qayyim was referring to the wider concept of khuluq, as explained above in the commentary, and not the narrow concept that is commonly held, namely that khuluq is only considered one's behavior toward others. - 2 lbn Rajah, Jaami, vol. 2, p. 98. But then ibn Rajah said that it is possible that al-birr here means performing the obligatory deeds while.al-taqwa refers to staying away from the forbidden acts.
content_id:
Komentar Umum tentang Hadis: Ini adalah hadis Nabi ﷺ lain yang penting dan komprehensif, di mana beliau telah menjelaskan esensi kebaikan dan kedurhakaan.

Biografi Perawi (I): Al-Nawwas ibn Saman — Al-Nawwas ibn Saman adalah seorang sahabat Nabi ﷺ. Beliau, bersama ayahnya, bertemu Nabi ﷺ bersama delegasi kaumnya. Beliau tinggal di Madinah selama satu tahun untuk belajar dari Nabi ﷺ. Beliau adalah salah satu dari Ahl As-Suffah (kaum Muslim miskin yang tinggal di masjid Nabi). Dalam Sahih Muslim dicatat bahwa beliau pernah berkata bahwa satu-satunya yang mencegahnya dari berhijrah ke Madinah adalah kebutuhannya untuk bertanya kepada Nabi ﷺ. Mereka yang berhijrah ke Madinah tidak banyak bertanya kepada Nabi ﷺ karena ketidaksetujuan umum terhadap terlalu banyak bertanya. Oleh karena itu, hanya mereka yang datang dari luar Madinalah yang bebas mengajukan pertanyaan kepada Nabi ﷺ. Setelah wafatnya Nabi ﷺ beliau tinggal di Syam Besar. Tujuh belas hadis telah dicatat atas wewenangnya. Muslim meriwayatkan tiga di antaranya.

Biografi Perawi (II): Wabisah ibn Ma'bad — Wabisah ibn Ma'bad mengunjungi Nabi ﷺ sebagai bagian dari sebuah delegasi pada tahun 9 H. Beliau terkenal karena kelembutannya dan kecenderungannya untuk banyak menangis. Sebelas hadis telah dicatat atas wewenangnya.

'Kebaikan adalah Akhlak yang Baik': Ibn Utsaimin menjelaskan bagian hadis ini berarti bahwa akhlak yang baik adalah salah satu aspek dari kebaikan. Namun, ini tampaknya tidak menangkap esensi pernyataan Nabi ﷺ. Tampaknya, makna dari frasa ini mirip dengan makna frasa-frasa yang dibahas sebelumnya, 'Haji adalah Arafah,' dan 'Agama adalah nasihat.' Makna di sini adalah, 'Aspek utama atau esensi dari kebaikan adalah akhlak yang baik.'

Makna Al-Birr (Kebaikan): Kata birr digunakan dalam dua pengertian. Yang pertama adalah memperlakukan orang lain dengan cara yang baik. Khususnya, seseorang harus memperlakukan orang tuanya dengan cara yang baik. Ini dikenal sebagai birr al-walidain. Kata itu, bagaimanapun, digunakan dalam arti yang lebih umum untuk berperilaku baik kepada siapa pun, bukan hanya orang tua. Kadang-kadang kata birr digunakan bersama dengan kata takwa. Misalnya, Allah berfirman dalam Al-Quran: 'Dan tolong-menolonglah kamu dalam (mengerjakan) kebajikan (al-birr) dan takwa' (Al-Maidah: 2). Ibn Rajab menyatakan bahwa dalam ayat ini al-birr merujuk pada berurusan dengan orang lain sementara al-takwa merujuk pada berurusan dengan Allah dengan benar melalui ketaatan kepada perintah-perintah-Nya. Jika ini adalah penggunaan yang dimaksudkan oleh Nabi ﷺ dalam pernyataan tertentu ini, maka hadis harus dipahami sebagai berarti, 'Akhlak yang baik sangat penting untuk hubungan yang baik dengan orang lain.'

Pengertian kedua di mana birr digunakan merujuk pada semua tindakan ibadah dan ketaatan kepada Allah, baik yang batin maupun yang lahir. Penggunaan ini ditemukan dalam ayat Al-Quran berikut.
content_ar: 6 lbn محمد ﷺ, p. 165. His complete discussion (as relied upon here) is found on pp. 165-1 67. 7 Al-Hilaali, Eeqaadh, p. 375. Commentary on the Forty الحديث of النووي General Comments About the الحديث This is another المهم and comprehensive الحديث of النبي ﷺ (ﷺ), wherein he has explained the essence of righteousness and sinfulness. About the Narrator (I): al-Nawwaas ibn Samaan Al-Nawwaas ibn Samaan was a Companion of النبي ﷺ (ﷺ). He, accompanying his الأب, met النبي ﷺ (ﷺ) with a delegation of his people. He lived in المدينة المنورة for one year to learn from النبي ﷺ (ﷺ). He was one of the ahl al-Suffah (the poor المسلمون who lived in النبي ﷺ's المسجد). In صحيح مسلم he is recorded to have said that the only thing that kept him from emigrating to المدينة المنورة was his need or want to ask النبي ﷺ (ﷺ) questions. Those who emigrated to المدينة المنورة did not ask النبي ﷺ (ﷺ) many questions بسبب the general disapproval of asking أيضاً many questions. ومن ثَم, only those who came from outside المدينة المنورة were free to put questions to النبي ﷺ (ﷺ). بعد the الموت of النبي ﷺ (ﷺ) he lived in Greater Syria. Seventeen الحديث have been recorded on his authority. مسلم recorded three of them. About the Narrator (II): Waabisah ibn Mabad Waabisah ibn Mabad and visited النبي ﷺ as part of a delegation in the year 9 A.H. He was well-known for his gentleness and his tendency to cry a great deal. Eleven الحديث have been recorded on his authority. "Righteousness is الخير الأخلاق." lbn Uthaimeen explains this portion of the الحديث to mean that الخير الأخلاق is one aspect of righteousness. 1 غير أن, this does not seem to capture the essence of النبي ﷺ's statement. It seems, instead, that the meaning of this phrase is similar to the meaning of the phrases discussed earlier, "The pilgrimage is Arafah," and "The religion is naseehah." The meaning here is, "The الرئيسي or الجوهري aspect of righteousness is الخير الأخلاق. "2 As noted below, depending on how one understands the meaning of the word birr ("righteousness"), the الحديث could be left in its literal sense. The 1 lbn Uthaimeen, Sharh Riyaadh, vol. 6, p. 1 83. 2 Cf., ibn Daqeeq al-Eid, Sharh, p. 72. الحديث #27: "Righteousness is الخير الأخلاق .. " religion is truly and completely "الخير الأخلاق" in the sense explained below.1 The Meaning of al-Birr ("Righteousness") The word birr is used in two senses. The أولاً is treating others in a الخير fashion. وبالأخص, ينبغي للمرء treat one's الوالدان in a الخير manner. This is known as birr al-waalidain. The word, though, is used in a more general sense to behave well toward anyone, not just one's الوالدان. Sometimes the word birr is used in conjunction with the word التقوى. For example, الله says in the القرآن الكريم, ... -.:__s) a:1\j N1 Á i)jL;.Jj "Help you بعضهم بعضاً in al-birr and al-التقوى" (al-Maaidah 2). lbn Raja
